project: Stock Prediction Platform — Full Training Pipeline Fix
description: Fix all blockers preventing a full end-to-end training run: numeric overflow in feature store, LINEAR_ONLY mode in training cronjob, missing FRED macro join in feature engineering, and Flink operator scaled to 0. Then trigger and validate a complete training run across all 30 tickers using non-linear models with FRED macro features included.

userStories.title: Fix feature store numeric overflow for extreme feature values
userStories.description: As a data engineer, I want feature store writes to handle extreme numeric values so that tickers like NVDA don't fail silently with a NumericValueOutOfRange error.
acceptanceCriteria: In ml/features/store.py, add a clamp/clip step before upserting: cap feature_value at +/- 1e9 (well within numeric(18,8) precision limit of ~9.99e9), or widen the column to numeric(24,8) via an Alembic migration
If using migration: create an Alembic migration that runs ALTER TABLE feature_store ALTER COLUMN feature_value TYPE numeric(24,8)
Re-run feature computation for NVDA manually to verify no NumericValueOutOfRange error: python -m ml.features.store --tickers NVDA
feature_store table has rows for NVDA after the fix
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Widened feature_store.feature_value to numeric(24,8) via direct ALTER TABLE (applied 2026-04-08). Added defense-in-depth clamp at ±9.9e15 in store.py. NVDA OBV ~35.6B now stores correctly. 48576 rows written for NVDA with no errors.

userStories.title: Add FRED macro data join to on-the-fly feature engineering path
userStories.description: As a data scientist, I want FRED macro features (14 series: DGS2, DGS10, T10Y2Y, etc.) joined into the feature matrix during training so the model can use macroeconomic signals.
acceptanceCriteria: In ml/pipelines/components/data_loader.py, add a function load_fred_macro_from_db(tickers, start_date, end_date, settings) that reads from feast_fred_macro and returns a dict[ticker -> DataFrame] with the 14 FRED columns indexed by date
In ml/pipelines/training_pipeline.py, after step 1c (FRED fetch+write), add step 1d that calls load_fred_macro_from_db and left-joins the FRED columns onto each ticker's data_dict DataFrame by date
In ml/pipelines/components/feature_engineer.py, the on-the-fly path preserves pre-joined FRED columns without overwriting them
feast_fred_macro table must be non-empty after a training run (verify: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM feast_fred_macro;)
Training feature matrix for each ticker contains at least 10 of the 14 FRED columns
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added load_fred_macro_from_db to data_loader.py. Added step 1d to training_pipeline.py joining 14 FRED columns into each ticker DataFrame. Updated feature_engineer.py docstring confirming pre-joined columns survive on-the-fly compute. Verified: 366 FRED rows in feast_fred_macro, 14/14 FRED cols in enriched df. FRED key is in ml-namespace secret (not storage-namespace). Fixed pandas read_sql warning.

userStories.title: Add --use-fred CLI flag and remove LINEAR_ONLY from training cronjob
userStories.description: As an ML engineer, I want the training pipeline CLI to expose a --use-fred flag and I want the production cronjob to use all model families, not just linear ones.
acceptanceCriteria: In ml/pipelines/training_pipeline.py, add --use-fred CLI arg (action=store_true, also enabled via USE_FRED=true env var); gate FRED fetch+join behind it
In k8s/ml/cronjob-training.yaml, add env var USE_FRED: 'true'

In k8s/ml/cronjob-training.yaml, remove the LINEAR_ONLY env var entry entirely (or set value to 'false') to enable tree, booster (xgboost/lightgbm/catboost), and distance/neural (knn, svr, mlp) model families
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added use_fred param to run_training_pipeline() and --use-fred CLI flag (also via USE_FRED env var). Gated steps 1c and 1d behind use_fred. Removed LINEAR_ONLY from cronjob-training.yaml, added USE_FRED=true. Verified: --use-fred in --help, param present in signature, LINEAR_ONLY absent from YAML.

userStories.title: Scale Flink Kubernetes operator back to 1 replica
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want the Flink Kubernetes operator running at 1 replica so that all 5 FlinkDeployments can have active JM/TM pods.
acceptanceCriteria: Create k8s/flink/flink-operator-scale.yaml as a Deployment manifest with metadata.name=flink-kubernetes-operator, metadata.namespace=flink, spec.replicas=1, and a selector matching app.kubernetes.io/name=flink-kubernetes-operator
Include a comment in the file explaining this overrides the Helm-installed operator that was scaled to 0
Commit and push; then apply directly since ArgoCD has no registered apps: kubectl apply -f stock-prediction-platform/k8s/flink/flink-operator-scale.yaml
kubectl get pods -n flink | grep flink-kubernetes-operator shows 1/1 Running within 60s
kubectl get flinkdeployment -n flink shows all 5 jobs with LIFECYCLE STATE=STABLE
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created k8s/flink/flink-operator-scale.yaml with replicas:1. Applied via kubectl apply (strategic merge). Operator pod 1/1 Running within 110s. FlinkDeployments transitioning RECONCILING->STABLE as operator reconciles each job.

userStories.title: Create and run a full non-linear training job for all 30 tickers
userStories.description: As an ML engineer, I want to trigger a complete training run with all model families and FRED features across all 30 tickers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create k8s/ml/job-full-training-nonlinear.yaml as a one-off Job mirroring cronjob-training.yaml but: name=full-training-nonlinear, LINEAR_ONLY removed, USE_FRED=true, --horizons 1,7,14,30, activeDeadlineSeconds: 43200 (12h), resources.requests: cpu=8000m memory=16Gi, resources.limits: cpu=16000m memory=32Gi
TICKERS env var lists all 30: NVDA,AMD,TSLA,META,AMZN,GOOGL,AVGO,AMAT,AXON,DXCM,ALGN,BLDR,BX,CEG,DVN,FANG,APA,ALB,CZR,BBWI,DAL,AAL,CCL,DHI,CF,ABNB,SCHW,CDNS,ADSK,ANET
Apply: kubectl apply -f stock-prediction-platform/k8s/ml/job-full-training-nonlinear.yaml
Verify started: kubectl get job -n ml full-training-nonlinear shows ACTIVE=1
Verify non-linear models training: kubectl logs -n ml -l job-name=full-training-nonlinear --tail=30 contains at least one of: random_forest, gradient_boosting, xgboost, lightgbm
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Validate completed training: non-linear models registered and FRED data populated
userStories.description: As an ML engineer, I want to confirm the full training run finished successfully with non-linear models in the registry and FRED macro data written to the database.
acceptanceCriteria: kubectl get job -n ml full-training-nonlinear shows COMPLETIONS=1/1 (poll every 2 min up to 12h)
kubectl logs -n ml -l job-name=full-training-nonlinear --tail=50 contains 'pipeline_complete' and 'status': 'completed'

kubectl exec -n storage deployment/postgresql -- psql -U stockuser -d stockdb -c "SELECT model_name, COUNT(*) FROM model_registry GROUP BY model_name ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 15;" shows non-linear model names (random_forest, xgboost, lightgbm, etc.)
kubectl exec -n storage deployment/postgresql -- psql -U stockuser -d stockdb -c "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM feast_fred_macro;" returns > 0
kubectl exec -n storage deployment/postgresql -- psql -U stockuser -d stockdb -c "SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT ticker) FROM feature_store;" returns >= 30
Typecheck passes
